OPPOSING PARTY IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES IMMEDIATELY IN COMPLIANCE WITH SF LOCAL RULE 2.7B. FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THIS WRITTEN ORDER MAY RESULT IN SANCTIONS OR CONTEMPT PROCEEDINGS. THE COURT NOTES THAT ANY PARTY CAN OBVIATE THE NEED FOR A CONTINUANCE OF A MOTION FOR LACK OF COURTESY COPIES BY SIMPLY LODGING A COMPLETE SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF ALL DOCUMENTS RELATED TO THE MOTION. ARGUED. Mr. Foley contests the tentative ruling and argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF FERNANDO ANDRADE AN INDIVIDUAL, SULEMA LOZANO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two.

Plaintiff's motion to compel further production of documents is granted. Defendant must produce all-nonprivileged documents in response to requests 1 through 59 by April 28, 2025. If there are privileged documents, a privilege log should be produced.

Defendant's boilerplate objections (e.g., vague, overbroad) lack merit and the cited discovery is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Plaintiff's "other vehicle" requests regarding the same alleged defects is proper. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 154 [explaining probative value of "other vehicle" evidence]; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334, 346-347 [noting intentionally inadequate "repair" of fuel pump based on defendant's prior knowledge].)

Defendant presents no competent evidence showing that the requests are unduly burdensome. No party sought sanctions.

Order is signed after the hearing.

Visiting Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/REQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Feb-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Ningjing Jiang, Esq. 310-933-4271 Quill and Arrow LLP for Plaintiff.
Michael Foley, Esq. 408-961-4593 Bowman and Brooke for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFs FERNANDO ANDRADE,and SULEMA LOZANO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to their set one production of documents is granted. Defendant American Honda must produce all non-privileged responsive documents and serve further responses confirming that it has done so no later than April 30, 2025. If American Honda withholds any documents on the grounds of privilege, the withheld documents must be identified in a privilege log. Based on the supplemental declaration of Roy Enav, the court deems this motion filed as of November 5, 2024 and thus it is timely filed. Plaintiffs have shown that they seek production of discovery relevant documents and American Honda has failed to show that production of those documents imposes an undue burden on it.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and must drop a hard copy of the proposed order in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
